commit.message:
Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add fly.toml for fly.io deployment

commit.message:
Add Turkish syllabification (heceleme) to both Python and HTML

Rule-based syllable splitting using TDK rules: the last consonant
between two vowels always goes with the next vowel. Handles all 6
standard rules including adjacent vowels and 3-consonant clusters.

- syllabify() function with 19 test cases from TDK examples
- Interactive visualization showing split points and applied rules
- Demo output now shows syllable breakdown (e.g. en-düst-ri)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix tournament wallet logging, rake flow to union, and settlement

Tournament fixes:
- Split buy-in and rake into separate wallet_transactions (clean audit trail)
- Fix prize payout category: settlement → prize (consistent with server)
- Fix unregister refund category: cashout → refund
- Remove stale $ symbol from error message
- Use log_wallet_transaction RPC instead of direct insert (server-side)
- Fix entity_type → wallet_type column name in server prize logging

Rake flow (union vs standalone):
- Tournament rake credited to union owner when club is in a union
- Standalone club rake goes directly to club owner
- Cash game rake in logRakeCollection now credits union/club owner wallet
- Full wallet_transaction audit trail for all rake credits

Settlement:
- Add executeUnionRakeBack() for weekly 90% rake distribution to clubs
- Union retains 10% and holds all BBJ + promo chips
- Debit/credit with full audit trail on both sides

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: clickable heritage sites, layer registry, PMTiles, historic map overlays

Replace heritage heatmap with clickable circle points backed by PMTiles
vector tiles. Add invisible pick-point layer for click detection on
heritage features. Wire MapLibre queryRenderedFeatures into deck.gl
click pipeline so PMTiles-rendered layers support selection and detail
panel display.

Additional changes:
- Layer registry pattern with normalizeFeature/getTooltip per layer
- useLayerDataManager hook for centralized data fetching
- PMTiles for heritage (145K) and permits (30K) datasets
- Historic map overlays from David Rumsey Collection
- Favicons and project documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fiscNettePer 0.55 → 0.639 : PS pension (9,1%) et non PS capital (17,2%)

La rente PER est une pension, pas un revenu du capital. Les PS applicables
sont CSG 8,3% + CRDS 0,5% + CASA 0,3% = 9,1%, avec abattement 10%
sur l'assiette IR. Pour TMI 30% : 27% + 9,1% = 36,1% → net 63,9%.

L'ancien taux (55%) confondait PS capital et PS pension, sous-estimant
les revenus PER de ~16%.

commit.message:
fix: prevent heatmap flicker and disappearance on dashboard (#373)

The heatmap treemap would briefly appear then vanish due to two issues:

1. Store unconditionally replaced tickers with API response — an empty
   response (timeout, yfinance outage) cleared good stale data, removing
   the treemap. Now preserves existing data when the refetch returns empty.

2. Before the first fetch completed, the component flashed "No heatmap
   data available" for one frame because loading started as false. Added
   an `initialized` flag to show skeleton until the first fetch finishes.

3. Changed containerRef watch to flush:'post' for reliable DOM measurement
   after the v-else branch renders.

Co-authored-by: Travis <travis@noreply.com>

fix: 修复OpenClaw安全扫描报告中的问题

## 🔒 修复的安全问题

1. **统一Node版本要求**
   - package.json要求: >=16.0.0 (之前是>=18.0.0)
   - 与SKILL.md保持一致
   - 确保文档和配置的一致性

2. **修复repository URL**
   - 更新为正确的GitHub仓库地址
   - 移除占位符URL

3. **添加ngrok安全警告**
   - 在DEBUG-GUIDE.md中添加明显的安全警告
   - 说明ngrok仅用于开发测试
   - 强调生产环境风险
   - 提供安全使用建议

4. **新增安全分析文档**
   - SECURITY-ANALYSIS.md
   - 说明VirusTotal可能的误报原因
   - 详细解释加密功能的正当用途
   - 提供安全验证方法

这些修复解决了OpenClaw安全扫描报告指出的不一致性问题。

fix(ci): 全5プラットフォームのCI/CDビルド失敗を修正

Android/iOS: ONNX Runtime v1.24.3のダウンロードURLを修正
- Android: GitHub Releases → Maven Central (onnxruntime-android .aar)
- iOS: GitHub Releases → Azure blob (pod-archive-onnxruntime-c .zip)
- FindOnnxRuntime.cmake: Android自動DL時のAAR展開・再配置ロジック追加

Windows: MSVC_RUNTIME_LIBRARY ジェネレータ式の修正
- 誤: "MultiThreadedDLL$<$<CONFIG:Debug>:Debug>" → MultiThreadedDLLDebug（無効値）
- 正: "MultiThreaded$<$<CONFIG:Debug>:Debug>DLL" → MultiThreadedDebugDLL（/MDd）

Linux/macOS: doc_source.cpp生成失敗を修正
- find_package(Python3)がgodot-cppのサブスコープ内のみで実行されていた
- トップレベルCMakeLists.txtにfind_package(Python3)を追加し
  target_doc_sources()呼び出し時にPython3_EXECUTABLEが利用可能に

constraints derived from event dates, causing React Hook Form to see
empty values and show required-field errors. Also improved

commit.message:
fix: sync .githooks/pre-commit — enable auto-bump + doc sync checks

.githooks/pre-commit was outdated (30 lines, test-only) while
core.hooksPath=.githooks, so auto-bump, CHANGELOG check, doc sync
and targeted test coverage check were all inactive. Synced to full
150-line version from .git/hooks/pre-commit.

Bump version 3.2.5 → 3.2.6.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(v22): revert to scene.background + backgroundRotation for parallax

SphereGeometry BackSide caused black circle (pole convergence).
Use scene.background (no geometry artifacts) with:
- backgroundRotation: subtle rotation based on camera position → parallax
- backgroundIntensity: 0.4 (zoomed in) → 0.8 (zoomed out)

Interval session fuelling, 20 miles, custom distance, duration chips

- Interval sessions: suppress in-session schedule entirely; add two notes
  explaining pre-session-only strategy and variable track session duration
  (e.g. 16-20 × 400m with recovery adds unpredictability — duration is an
  estimate, per-hour target remains valid)
- Session subtype hint: show inline tip below dropdown when 'Intervals' is
  selected reminding user to fuel pre-session
- Race distances: add '20 miles (32.2 km)' option; update paceUtils and
  fuelEngine Distance type accordingly
- Custom distance: when 'Other / custom' is selected show a km number input;
  pace estimator uses this value so duration can still be calculated
- Duration quick-fill chips: context-aware row of common durations
  (session: 0:30 / 0:45 / 1:00 / 1:15 / 1:30 / 2:00,
   race: 0:45 / 1:00 / 1:30 / 2:00 / 3:00 / 4:00) — click to fill field

commit.message:
feat: add remote Supabase auth fallback when JWT secret unavailable

auth.py now has two verification strategies:
1. Local HS256 (fast, zero network calls) when SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET is set
2. Remote via Supabase /auth/v1/user (one call) as fallback

This allows auth to work immediately without needing the JWT secret.

commit.message:
Fase 8.G fix: pre-cargar glifos ASCII para evitar freeze/kick del servidor

SFML construye el atlas de glifos on-demand la primera vez que dibuja
cada caracter. Con 11 funciones PutString usando 6 fuentes distintas,
el primer frame generaba un freeze de varios segundos, causando que
el servidor kickeara al cliente por timeout.

Fix: pre-cargar glifos ASCII (32-126) para cada fuente/tamano/estilo
durante Init(), moviendo el trabajo costoso a la inicializacion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: Add XSS regression prevention checks and documentation

Added safeguards to prevent reintroduction of XSS vulnerabilities:

1. scripts/check-xss-patterns.sh
   - Automated check for innerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML with string interpolation
   - Detects onclick handlers with ID injection (+ operator in strings)
   - Safe for manual testing and CI integration

2. .git/hooks/pre-commit
   - Git pre-commit hook runs XSS pattern check before commits
   - Blocks commits with XSS-prone patterns
   - Can be bypassed with --no-verify (flags potential security issue)

3. CLAUDE.md - XSS Prevention Pattern documentation
   - Added critical security pattern section
   - Shows vulnerable vs safe DOM rendering code
   - Explains textContent vs innerHTML distinction
   - Documents closure pattern for event handlers
   - Links to automated regression check

This prevents accidental reintroduction of:
- innerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML with user data interpolation
- onclick handlers with ID/data string injection
- Any future DOM-based XSS patterns

Static onclick handlers (with hardcoded literals) are allowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(lexer): ✨ add :: (ColonColon) token and capture design philosophy

## Summary

Add `::` as a first-class lexer token for namespace/module path
qualification, distinct from `:` (type annotation / block introducer).
Capture long-term design doctrine covering stdlib posture, module
system, and GPU-through-modes strategy in `language_vision.md`.

## Highlights

- Add `ColonColon` variant to `TokenKind` with lookahead disambiguation
from `Colon`
- Update `spec/grammar/dao.lex`, `CONTRACT_SYNTAX_SURFACE.md`, and
`CONTRACT_LANGUAGE_TOOLING.md`
- Add `operator.namespace` to the frozen semantic token taxonomy
- Add `Namespace Qualification` section to syntax surface contract
establishing `::` vs `.` distinction
- Expand `language_vision.md` with design doctrine, stdlib layering
(small stdlib / large platform), module/namespace design (`::` paths,
visibility tiers), and GPU-through-modes strategy
- Add `ARCH_INDEX.md` entry for `language_vision.md`
- Add disambiguation test verifying `:` vs `::` in mixed contexts (`x:
int32` + `net::http::get`)

## Test plan

- [x] All existing lexer tests pass
- [x] New `colon vs coloncolon disambiguation` test validates mixed `:`
/ `::` contexts
- [x] Updated `all operators recognized` test covers 22 operators
including `ColonColon`
- [x] `daoc lex` correctly emits `ColonColon` for `import net::http`
- [ ] CI build + test pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: client feedback — rule enforcement, tool exclusion, spinner overlap, loop detection (#819) (#824)

* fix(services): convert security-baseline rules from md to yaml with hard enforcement (#819)

The built-in security-baseline pack rules (no-eval, parameterized-queries,
no-hardcoded-secrets) shipped as .md files with no metadata. Since
_read_artifact_content() only extracts metadata from .yaml files, the rules
had empty metadata and were treated as soft-enforced (no enforcement effect).

Converts all three rules to YAML format with enforce: hard metadata,
match patterns, and block reasons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): add --denied-tools flag for hard-blocking tools (#820)

Adds --denied-tools CLI flag parallel to --allowed-tools. Denied tools are
hard-blocked without any approval prompt. This lets users exclude specific
tools without using allowed_tools (which auto-approves and bypasses the
approval gate).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): stop thinking spinner before showing approval prompt (#823)

The thinking spinner background ticker was still running when
_confirm_destructive() displayed the approval prompt, causing the
spinner text to overlap/cover the approval text. Adds stop_thinking()
before and start_thinking() after the prompt, matching the existing
pattern in _ask_user_callback().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(services): detect line repetition during streaming, not just post-stream (#821)

Previously, line repetition was only checked after the full response
streamed, meaning all repeated lines were already rendered to the user.
Now tracks repetition inline during token emission and breaks out of
streaming early when the threshold is hit, reducing visible repetition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: fix lint and format issues (#819)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add --denied-tools to CLAUDE.md and a-help skill (#820)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(services): address code review feedback (#819)

- Fix yaml.load regex false positive in no-eval rule (negative lookahead placement)
- Move import json to module level in test_starter_packs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(services): include file content in rule enforcement and fix yaml.load regex (#819)

- _stringify_arguments() now includes content/new_text for write_file/edit_file
- yaml.load regex requires SafeLoader specifically, not just any Loader
- .env path pattern updated to work with path\ncontent format
- 13 new tests for content matching and yaml.load variants

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Classify StructTypeKeyedPattern → §10.9.2 [skip ci]

The test uses `mixed_t'{int: 32'd99, default: 8'd0}` — a structure assignment pattern with a type name (`int`) as a key. LRM §10.9.2 "Structure assignment patterns" explicitly covers type-keyed patterns, stating "a type can be used as the key" to override the default for members of that type, which is exactly the construct under test.

commit.message:
fix(critical): resolve useAuth timing race and RPC column mismatch

Two bugs that caused complete app failure:

1. useAuth timing race: fallbackLoading initialized to false, so between
   useUser finishing and the fallback useEffect firing, there was a render
   where isLoading=false + company=null. Pages rendered "Unable to load
   workspace data" during this gap. Fixed by tracking fallbackDone instead -
   pendingCompany stays true until fallback completes or is unnecessary.

2. RPC crash: get_enhanced_dashboard_stats referenced leases.monthly_rent
   but the actual column is leases.rent_amount. The RPC threw a 42703 error
   on every call, preventing dashboard stats from loading. Fixed in SQL and
   added bulletproof direct-query fallback that wraps every table query in
   a safe() wrapper so a single missing table never crashes the dashboard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
